framework.name: AP2 Liability Model: Accountability anchored to real-world entities, never the agent
framework.description: AP2 explicitly assigns accountability to users, merchants, or issuers — never the agent itself. Disputes resolved via cryptographic audit trails (signed mandates). For HNP flows, the user's pre-signed Intent Mandate serves as non-repudiable proof of intent, meaning the user bears responsibility for what they authorized the agent to do within defined conditions.
framework.raw_quote: Accountability tied to real-world entities such as the user, merchant or the issuer, and not the agent. Cryptographic audit trails are designed to ensure that disputes are resolved with verifiable evidence.

source.title: PayPal Community Blog | Agent Payments Protocol: Building Verifiable Trust for Agentic Commerce
